reviews.review_text: I don't know how it did it, but this game made my laptop's monitor burned out once, I have to buy retold version to play it, maybe I have to remove this game from my library to protect my laptop
A classic, has some better mechanics compared to previous AoEs, with a nice mythological theme.
I like the mythological aspect of the game. stems from real life gods. very nice

I have been trying to get this game working since 2024 and no luck. It now won't load anymore despite almost a hundred hours of playtime. A real shame. The weird thing was it stopped working a little after the new one came out.
I don't know if it is related but the timing was terrible. Wish I could play more. : (

This is a pure classic, played this as a child and still play it as an adult. If it's your first time ever seeing this series you should look towards the new and improved AOM: Retold as it has tons of quality of life changes and improvements.
Its a very fun game. I reccomend if you are playing for long periods of time and you get bored hit enter and type BAWK BAWK BOOM. just like that.
Another classic from my childhood that I loved playing when visiting the cabin on vacation on CD-ROM. The music is amazing and really brings me back to that time.

Retold is the same price. Play that instead or wait til this is on-sale. For a newbie who never played these games I'd recommend just doing Retold. This game is great and all growing up with it. Today you'll feel the jank if you're not use to it

If your specs can't run Retold this is a good buy. TOTD Chinese faction isn't as cool as Retold's. I have complaints about Retold but still recommend that over this one
This version is essentially outdated now that Age of Mythology: Retold is out, but I do still like to come back to it every once in awhile.

The absolute peak of childhood tactical fantasy where history steps aside to let the gods throw meteors.

While the mainline Age of Empires titles focused on the meticulous details of human history, Ensemble Studios looked at the formula in 2002 and asked, "What if we let players summon a Kraken?" The result was Age of Mythology: Extended Edition, a flawless, highly imaginative masterpiece that captures the pure magic of childhood mythology obsession. The transition to Steam brings full Steamworks integration, global cloud saves, and a beautiful enhanced water and lighting engine. Firing it up today instantly triggers a massive wave of nostalgia, from the iconic, booming Egyptian theme music to Arkantos’ dramatic, world-spanning campaign across Atlantis, Greece, Egypt, and the frozen north.

The gameplay loop is an absolute masterclass in mechanical variety and destructive spectacle. Instead of standard civilizational upgrades, every time you advance to a new age, you choose a minor deity to worship—like Anubis, Thor, or Aphrodite—which completely shifts your tactical options. You aren't just micro-managing standard spearmen and archers; you are commanding terrifying Myth Units like towering Cyclopes, laser-shooting Sphinxes, and frost giants. The absolute crown jewel of the system, however, is the God Powers. Having the ability to completely flatten an entire enemy base with a well-timed Meteor storm, tear a hole in reality with an Underworld Passage, or summon the literal Son of Osiris to shoot lightning from his hands provides a level of sheer, high-dopamine power that no standard historical RTS can dream of replicating.

Decades later, even with newer iterations on the horizon, the Extended Edition stands as a pristine, incredibly robust package. It seamlessly bundles the classic base game with the game-changing The Titans expansion—which lets you build giant, map-altering super-units that erupt from the earth—and the Golden Gift campaign. The Steam Workshop support keeps the engine incredibly alive, allowing you to easily inject community texture packs, completely reworked campaigns, and crazy custom scenarios. It is a wonderfully chaotic, deeply nostalgic, and endlessly replayable "no-brainer" classic that runs beautifully on modern PCs. Proving that some legends truly never die, it remains an essential cornerstone of strategy gaming history.

10/10 — My opponent sent a massive, perfectly organized army of elite Greek hoplites to my base. I clicked the 'Ragnarok' button, turned all my villagers into glowing heroes, called down a lightning storm, and won the game. Prostagma?
this is one of the best games ever and passes time away
